inputs.userGoal: Provision an event-driven data processing pipeline using Azure Container Apps with Dapr Pub/Sub integration, connected to Azure Event Grid for event ingestion, and outputting processed results to Cosmos DB.

architecturePrinciples: Event-driven and serverless — scale to zero when idle
Loosely coupled via Dapr Pub/Sub abstraction
Pay-per-use consumption billing across all services
Observable via centralized Log Analytics logging

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by Container Apps Environment for centralized application and platform logging. PerGB2018 is the recommended pay-as-you-go tier for new deployments.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pay-per-GB ingestion cost; 30-day retention balances cost with diagnostic utility.

resources.name: cae-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Consumption workload profile provides serverless scale-to-zero for event-driven workloads with no minimum cost. Supports Dapr components for Pub/Sub and state store integration natively.
alternativesConsidered: Dedicated workload profiles (D4/E4) — unnecessary for event-driven burst workloads
AKS — higher operational overhead for this scenario
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption profile has cold-start latency; acceptable for async data processing pipelines.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Log Analytics workspace must exist before the environment to configure log ingestion via customerId and sharedKey.

resources.name: evgt-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Event Grid provides serverless event routing with CloudEvents schema for interoperability with Dapr Pub/Sub. Basic SKU is sufficient without private endpoint requirements.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Service Bus — higher cost, better for ordered messaging but unnecessary here
Event Hubs — optimized for high-throughput telemetry streaming, overkill for event-driven pipeline triggers
reasoning.tradeoffs: Basic SKU lacks private endpoints; acceptable for initial deployment. CloudEvents v1.0 schema aligns with Dapr's native pub/sub format.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No dependencies — standalone event ingestion resource.

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB with Serverless capacity and NoSQL API provides pay-per-request pricing ideal for bursty event-driven writes. Session consistency balances performance and read-your-writes guarantees. Single-region aligns with serverless constraint.
alternativesConsidered: Provisioned throughput Cosmos DB — unnecessary fixed cost for variable event loads
Azure SQL — relational model not needed for pipeline output documents
Azure Table Storage — lacks indexing flexibility and query power
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only with no multi-region writes; acceptable for a single-region pipeline. No shared-throughput databases.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001/pipeline-db

reasoning.whyChosen: SQL database container for organizing processed event data. No shared throughput since the account is serverless.
alternativesConsidered: Multiple databases — unnecessary for single-pipeline workload

resources.dependencyReasoning: Database is a child resource of the Cosmos DB account.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001/pipeline-db/processed-events

reasoning.whyChosen: Container to store processed event output documents. Partition key on /partitionKey allows flexible routing by event source or type.
alternativesConsidered: Hierarchical partition key — adds complexity without clear benefit at this scale
reasoning.tradeoffs: Generic partition key path; can be refined based on actual query patterns.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Container is a child resource of the SQL database.

resources.name: ca-eventprocessor-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Container App with Dapr enabled for Pub/Sub provides native event-driven scaling and loose coupling to Event Grid and Cosmos DB via Dapr components. Scale-to-zero minimizes cost when idle. 0.5 CPU / 1Gi is a valid consumption-tier combination.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Functions — less control over runtime, but viable alternative
AKS pod — higher operational overhead for single-service pipeline
reasoning.tradeoffs: Placeholder container image must be replaced with actual processor image. Dapr Pub/Sub and state store components are configured on the environment (not in this resource).

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires the Container Apps Environment for hosting, Event Grid topic for event subscription, and Cosmos DB account for output writes via Dapr bindings.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture uses a fully serverless, event-driven approach. Azure Event Grid ingests events via a custom topic with CloudEvents schema. A Dapr-enabled Container App on a Consumption-tier environment subscribes to events via Dapr Pub/Sub, processes them, and writes results to a Serverless Cosmos DB NoSQL account. Log Analytics provides centralized observability. All resources use pay-per-use pricing to minimize idle costs.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: All resources are single-region (eastus) with no zone redundancy or private networking — suitable for development/initial production but should be hardened for mission-critical workloads. Serverless Cosmos DB is single-region only. The placeholder container image must be replaced with the actual event processor application.

plan.validation: All resources deploy to eastus. Container App references the Environment via environmentId. Environment connects to Log Analytics via customerId/sharedKey. Cosmos DB serverless is single-region with Session consistency (no constraint violations). Event Grid Basic SKU with CloudEvents schema is compatible with Dapr Pub/Sub. CPU/memory combination (0.5/1Gi) is valid for Consumption profile. No subnet or network conflicts as no VNet integration is configured.
